title: OpenAI Images v2 rolling out to all ChatGPT accounts
summary: OpenAI Images v2, an upgraded image generation capability, is now available across all ChatGPT tiers. Confirmed via Logan Kilpatrick on X.

title: NZ AI skills demand quadruples; older workers outperforming juniors on AI adoption
summary: Demand for AI-related skills in New Zealand has quadrupled in job ads over the past decade, and workers over 55 are outperforming younger colleagues in AI adoption rates. Despite 44% of NZ workers using AI daily, only 13% have received formal training — a gap Microsoft aims to close with 200,000 upskilling spots.

title: NZ workers not actively choosing an AI future — just clicking accept
summary: A Newsroom analysis argues New Zealanders are defaulting into an AI-dependent future through passive consent clicks rather than deliberate policy choices. Frames AI adoption as a governance gap — organisations and individuals face AI exposure they have not consciously evaluated.

title: Logan Kilpatrick: Google Cloud is entering the 'agent cloud' era
summary: Logan Kilpatrick (Google AI Studio) described Google Cloud's direction at Cloud Next as the 'agent cloud' — positioning Google Cloud not just as infrastructure but as the orchestration layer for agentic enterprise workflows. Google CEO Thomas Kurian cited this as the company's core strategic direction.

title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
summary: Google and NVIDIA demonstrate Gemma 4 running as a Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model on the Jetson Orin Nano Super, enabling on-device robot perception and planning. Gemma 4 proves capable as a lightweight foundation model for physical AI applications on constrained edge hardware.

title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
summary: NVIDIA's Earth Day roundup covers five production deployments of AI-accelerated computing across climate modelling, biodiversity conservation, disaster monitoring, and recycling-plant automation. Documents real deployments rather than research demos.

title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA and Google Cloud deepened their decade-long joint platform spanning every technology layer from optimised libraries to enterprise cloud services, enabling developers to push agentic and physical AI from lab to production. The collaboration targets robotics, autonomous systems, and large-scale agent deployment pipelines.

title: Google launches eighth-generation TPUs: 8T for training, 8I for inference
summary: Google unveiled two specialized eighth-generation TPUs at Cloud Next 2026 — the 8T (training) and 8I (inference) — each delivering 2-3x performance over the previous generation and purpose-built for the agentic era. Logan Kilpatrick (Google AI Studio) called them 'Google's secret sauce' now available through Cloud.

title: AI is handling 1,000+ NZ job interviews a month — humans still carry all the legal risk
summary: AI tools are conducting over 1,000 job interviews per month in New Zealand, but legal and ethical accountability remains entirely with human hiring managers. Examines the widening gap between AI deployment in HR and the employment law frameworks governing those decisions.

title: Sam Altman confirms Codex stays on free and Plus plans
summary: Sam Altman confirmed Codex will remain accessible on free and Plus ($20/month) ChatGPT plans, directly addressing user concerns that Codex access was being restricted. Extends Codex's reach to millions of individual developers beyond enterprise tiers.

title: NZ Government launches national survey on business AI adoption
summary: New Zealand government launches a national survey of businesses to measure AI adoption rates and gauge responses to economic and technological change. Data will directly inform implementation of the MBIE AI Strategy.

title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
summary: DeepMind introduces Decoupled DiLoCo, a distributed training method separating gradient computation from parameter updates for improved resilience on heterogeneous hardware. Enables efficient LLM training across commodity clusters without tight synchronisation.

title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
summary: OpenAI details how WebSocket connections and connection-scoped caching in the Responses API reduced API overhead and improved model latency inside the Codex agent loop. A practical deep dive for builders running long-horizon agentic workflows at scale.

title: Employment Hero launches HeroForce: AI co-employment model for NZ SMEs
summary: Employment Hero launched HeroForce, an AI-powered co-employment model designed to simplify payroll, HR admin, and employment law compliance for New Zealand SMEs. Targets the country's complex regulatory environment as a commercial obstacle being solved through AI automation.

title: Health NZ develops BroPilot: culturally-grounded Māori version of Microsoft Copilot
summary: Hauora Māori Services developed BroPilot, a version of Microsoft Copilot incorporating te reo Māori and tikanga principles for Māori health contexts. Addresses a critical gap in AI tools for indigenous healthcare and demonstrates a model for culturally-responsive AI adaptation.
